pattern: One pass tallies how often each character occurs, and a second pass returns the index of the first character whose tally is exactly one.
hints: Uniqueness is a global property, so you cannot decide it for a character until you have seen the whole string.
Count every character's frequency first, then revisit the string in order looking for a count of one.
The first index whose character has frequency one is the answer; if none qualifies, return -1.

edge_inputs.description: last character is the only unique one

stress.description: random lowercase string over a tiny alphabet so duplicates are common
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [''.join(rng.choice('abcd') for _ in range(max(1, size)))]
stress.note: tiny alphabet stresses the no-unique branch and ordering
